exactText: מִבַּ֖ﬠַד רַקָּתֵ֔ךְ הָֽרִמּוֹן֙ כְּפֶ֤לַח תָּל֣וּי הַמָּגֵן֙ אֶ֤לֶף לְתַלְפִּיּ֑וֹת בָּנ֖וּי צַוָּארֵ֔ךְ דָּוִיד֙ כִּשְׁנֵ֥י שָׁדַ֛יִךְ שְׁנֵ֥י הַגִּבֹּרִֽים׃ שִׁלְטֵ֥י כֹּ֖ל ﬠָלָ֔יו ﬠַ֤ד בַּשּֽׁוֹשַׁנִּֽים׃ הָֽרוֹﬠִ֖ים צְבִיָּ֑ה תְּאוֹמֵ֣י ﬠֳפָרִ֖ים אֶל־הַ֣ר לִי֙ אֵ֤לֶךְ הַצְּלָלִ֑ים וְנָ֖סוּ הַיּ֔וֹם שֶׁיָּפ֨וּחַ֙ רַﬠְיָתִ֔י יָפָה֙ כֻּלָּ֤ךְ הַלְּבוֹנָֽה׃ וְאֶל־גִּבְﬠַ֖ת הַמּ֔וֹר בָּֽךְ׃ אֵ֥ין וּמ֖וּם 4:1–7) Behold, thou art fair, my love; behold, thou art fair; thine eyes are as doves behind thy veil; thy hair is as a flock of goats, that trail down from mount Gilead. Thy teeth are like a flock of ewes all shaped alike, which are come up from the washing; whereof all are paired, and none faileth among them. Thy lips are like a thread of scarlet, and thy mouth is comely; thy temples are like a pomegranate split open behind thy veil. Thy neck is like the tower of David builded with turrets, whereon there hang a thousand shields, all the armour of the mighty men.

exactText: Thy two breasts are like two fawns that are twins of a gazelle, which feed among the lilies. Until the day breathe, and the shadows flee away , I will get me to the mountain of myrrh, and to the hill of frankincense. Thou art all fair, my love; and there is no spot in thee. לְקוֹלֵ֖ מַקְשִׁיבִ֥ים חֲבֵרִ֛ים בַּגַּנִּ֗ים הַיּוֹשֶׁ֣בֶת א֚וֹ לִצְבִי֙ וּֽדְמֵה־לְךָ֤ דּוֹדִ֗י ׀ בְּרַ֣ח הַשְׁמִיﬠִֽנִי׃ בְשָׂמִֽים׃ הָרֵ֥י ﬠַ֖ל הָֽאַיָּלִ֔ים לְﬠֹ֣פֶר 8:13–14) Thou that dwellest in the gardens, the companions hearken for thy voice: ‘Cause me to hear it.’ Make haste, my beloved, and be thou like to a gazelle or to a young hart upon the mountains of spices. ~ 67 ~

exactText: Ki Lo Na’eh —&כי&לו&נאהFor to God, it is fitting אAdir bi-m’luchah, בbaḥur ka-halachah, ג gedudav yom’ru lo: ,בִּמְלוּכָה אַדִּיר ,כַּהֲלָכָה בָּחוּר לוֹ׃ יֹאמְרוּ גְּדוּדָיו Refrain L’cha u-l’cha, l’cha ki l’cha, l’cha af l’cha, l’cha YHWH ha-mamlacha. פזמון,לְךָ כִּי לְךָ , וּלְךָ לְךָ .הַמַּמְלָכָה יְיָ לְךָ , לְךָ אַף לְךָ Ki lo na’eh, ki lo ya’eh. יָאֶה׃ לוֹ כִּי , נָאֶה לוֹ כִּי דDagul bi-m’luchah, הhadur ka-halachah, ו vaAkav yom’ru lo: Refrain ,בִּמְלוּכָה דָּגוּל ,כַּהֲלָכָה הָדוּר לוֹ׃ יֹאמְרוּ וָתִיקָיו פזמון ז Zakai bi-m’luchah, חḥasin ka-halachah, טtaf’serav yom’ru lo: Refrain ,בִּמְלוּכָה זַכַּאי ,כַּהֲלָכָה חָסִין לוֹ׃ יֹאמְרוּ טַפְסְרָיו פזמון י Yaḥid bi-m’luchah, כkabir ka-halachah, לlimudav yom’ru lo: Refrain ,בִּמְלוּכָה יָחִיד ,כַּהֲלָכָה כַּבִּיר לוֹ׃ יֹאמְרוּ לִמּוּדָיו פזמון מMarom bi-m’luchah, נ nora ka-halachah, סsevivav yom’ru lo: Refrain ,בִּמְלוּכָה מָרוֹם ,כַּהֲלָכָה נוֹרָא לוֹ׃ יֹאמְרוּ סְבִיבָיו פזמון עAnav bi-m’luchah, פpodeh ka-halachah, צtzadikav yom’ru lo: Refrain ,בִּמְלוּכָה ﬠָנָיו ,כַּהֲלָכָה פּוֹדֶה לוֹ׃ יֹאמְרוּ צַדִּיקָיו פזמון קKadosh bi-m’luchah, רraḥum ka-halachah, שshin’anav yom’ru lo: Refrain ,בִּמְלוּכָה קָדוֹשׁ ,כַּהֲלָכָה רַחוּם לוֹ׃ יֹאמְרוּ שִׁנְאַנָּיו פזמון תTakif bi-m’luchah, תtomeich ka-halachah, תtemimav yom’ru lo: Refrain ,בִּמְלוּכָה תַּקִּיף ,כַּהֲלָכָה תּוֹמֵךְ לוֹ׃ יֹאמְרוּ תְּמִימָיו פזמון ~ 68 ~

exactText: אMighty in sovereignty , ב distinguished on God’s path, ג God’s divine retinue will declare: Refrain To You, and for You, To You, because of You, To You, particularly to You, To You, God, belongs sovereignty . For to God, it is fitting, For to God, it shall be fitting. ד Exalted in sovereignty , הglorious on God’s path, ו God’s faithful ones will declare: Refrain ז Faultless in sovereignty , חkind on God’s path, טGod’s appointed ones will declare: Refrain י Unique in sovereignty , כ illustrious on God’s path, ל God’s wise ones will declare: Refrain מSupreme in sovereignty , נ awe-inspiring on God’s path, סthose who surround God will declare: Refrain עHumble in sovereignty , פ honorable on God’s path, צ God’s righteous ones will declare: Refrain קHoly in sovereignty , ר merciful on God’s path, שGod’s followers will declare: Refrain תResolute in sovereignty , תsupportive on God’s path, תGod’s perfect ones will declare: Refrain ~ 69 ~

exactText: Adir Hu&—&אדיר&הואMighty is God א Mighty is God, Adir hu, ,הוּא אַדִּיר Refrain Yiv’neh veito be-karov, bi-m’heirah, bi-m’heirah, be-yameinu ve-karov, eil, b’nei, eil, b’nei, b’nei veit’cha be-karov. פזמון,בְּקָרוֹב בֵיתוֹ יִבְנֶה ,בִּמְהֵרָה , בִּמְהֵרָה ,בְקָרוֹב בְּיָמֵינוּ ,בְּנֵה , אֵל , בְּנֵה , אֵל בְּקָרוֹב׃ בֵיתְךָ בְּנֵה May God build God’s dwelling place speedily , in our days. God, rebuild Your dwelling place! ב Distinguished is God, ג great is God, ד exalted is God, Refrain Baḥur hu, gadol hu, dagul hu, Refrain ,הוּא בָּחוּר ,הוּא גָּדוֹל ,הוּא דָּגוּל פזמון הGlorious is God, ו faithful is God, ז faultless is God, Refrain Hadur hu, vaAk hu, zakai hu, Refrain ,הוּא הָדוּר ,הוּא וָתִיק ,הוּא זַכַּאי פזמון חKind is God, טpure is God, י unique is God, Refrain Ḥasid hu, tahor hu, yaḥid hu, Refrain ,הוּא חָסִיד ,הוּא טָהוֹר ,הוּא יָחִיד פזמון כ Powerful is God, ל wise is God, מsovereign is God,Refrain Kabir hu, lamud hu, melech hu, Refrain ,הוּא כַּבִּיר ,הוּא לָמוּד ,הוּא מֶֽלֶךְ פזמון נ Illuminating is God, סsublime is God, עpowerful is God, Refrain Na’or hu, sagiv hu, izuz hu, Refrain ,הוּא נָאוֹר ,הוּא סַגִּיב ,הוּא ﬠִזּוּז פזמון פ Honorable is God, צ righteous is God, קholy is God, Refrain Podeh hu, tzadik hu, kadosh hu, Refrain ,הוּא פּוֹדֶה ,הוּא צַדִיק ,הוּא קָּדוֹשׁ פזמון ר Merciful is God, שalmighty is God, תpreeminent is God,Refrain Raḥum hu, shaddai hu, takif hu, Refrain ,הוּא רַחוּם ,הוּא שַׁדַּי ,הוּא תַּקִּיף פזמון ~ 70 ~

exactText: Eḥad Mi Yodei’a&—&אחד&מי&יודעWho knows one? 1:Eḥad mi yode’a? Eḥad ani yode’a: 1 Eḥad eloheinu, she-ba-shamayim u-va-aretz. ? יוֹדֵֽעַ מִי אֶחָד יוֹדֵֽעַ׃ אֲנִי אֶחָד וּבָאָֽרֶץ׃ שֶׁבַּשָּׁמַיִם , אֱלֹהֵֽינוּ אֶחָד 2:Sh’nayim mi yode’a? Sh’nayim ani yode’a: 2 Sh’nei luḥot ha-b’rit, 1 Eḥad eloheinu, she-ba-shamayim u-va-aretz. יוֹדֵֽעַ׃ אֲנִי שְׁנַֽ יִם ? יוֹדֵֽעַ מִי שְׁנַֽ יִם ,הַבְּרִית לֻחוֹת שְׁנֵי וּבָאָֽרֶץ׃ שֶׁבַּשָּׁמַֽיִם , אֱלֹהֵֽינוּ אֶחָד 13:Sh’loshah asar mi yode’a? Sh’loshah asar ani yode’a: 13Sh’loshah asar midaya, 12Sh’neim asar shiv’taya, 11Aḥad asar koch’vaya, 10Asarah dib’raya, 9 Tish’ah yar’ḥei leidah, 8 Sh’monah y’mei milah, 7 Shiv’ah y’mei shab’ta, 6 Shishah sid’rei mishnah, 5 Ḥamishah ḥum’shei torah, 4 Ar’ba imahot, 3 Sh’loshah avot, 2 Sh’nei luḥot ha-b’rit, 1 Eḥad eloheinu, she-ba-shamayim u-va-aretz. ? יוֹדֵֽעַ מִי ﬠָשָׂר שְׁלשָׁה יוֹדֵֽעַ׃ אֲנִי ﬠָשָׂר שְׁלשָׁה ,מִדַּיָא ﬠָשָׂר שְׁלשָׁה ,שִׁבְטַיָא ﬠָשָׂר שְׁנֵים ,כּוֹכְבַיָּא ﬠָשָׂר אַחַד ,דִבְּרַיָא ﬠֲשָׂרָה ,לֵדָה יַרְחֵי תִּשְׁﬠָה ,מִילָה יְמֵי שְׁמוֹנָה ,שַׁבָּתָא יְמֵי שִׁבְﬠָה ,מִשְׁנָה סִדְרֵי שִׁשָּׁה ,תוֹרָה חוּמְשֵׁי חֲמִשָׁה ,אִמָהוֹת אַרְבַּע ,אָבוֹת שְׁלֹשָׁה ,הַבְּרִית לֻחוֹת שְׁנֵי וּבָאָֽרֶץ׃

exactText: שֶׁבַּשָּׁמַֽיִם , אֱלֹהֵֽינוּ אֶחָד Who knows one? I know one: One is our God, in heaven and earth. Who knows thirteen? I know thirteen: Thirteen are the attributes of God. Twelve are the tribes of Israel. Eleven are the stars in Joseph’s dream. Ten are the commandments. Nine are the months of pregnancy . Eight are the days before b’rit milah. Seven are the days of the week. Six are the orders of Mishnah. Five are the books of Torah. Four are the matriarchs. Three are the patriarchs. Two are the Tablets of the Covenant. One is our God, in heaven and earth. ~ 71 ~

exactText: Ḥad Gad’ya — חד&גדיאOne little kid One little kid, that my father bought for two zuzim, ḥad gad’ya. Ḥad gadya, ḥad gadya, di-z’van abba bi-t’rei zuzei, ḥad gadya, ḥad gadya. ,גַּדְיָא חַד , גַּדְיָא חַד ,זוּזֵי בִּתְרֵי אַבָּא דִּזְבַן גַּדְיָא׃ חַד , גַּדְיָא חַד Then came a cat, that ate the kid, that my father bought for two zuzim, ḥad gad’ya. Ve-ata shun’ra, ve-achal le-gadya, di-z’van abba bi-t’rei zuzei, ḥad gadya, ḥad gadya. ,שׁוּנְרָא וְאָתָא ,לְגַדְיָא וְאָכַל ,זוּזֵי בִּתְרֵי אַבָּא דִּזְבַן גַּדְיָא׃ חַד , גַּדְיָא חַד Then came a dog, that bit the cat, that ate the kid, that my father bought for two zuzim, ḥad gad’ya. Ve-ata chal’ba, ve-nashach le-shun’ra, de-achal le-gadya, di-z’van abba bi-t’rei zuzei, ḥad gadya, ḥad gadya. ,כַלְבָּא וְאָתָא ,לְשׁוּנְרָא וְנָשַׁךְ ,לְגַדְיָא דְּאָכַל ,זוּזֵי בִּתְרֵי אַבָּא דִּזְבַן גַּדְיָא׃

exactText: חַד , גַּדְיָא חַד Then came a stick, that beat the dog, that ate the kid, that my father bought for two zuzim, ḥad gad’ya. Ve-ata ḥut’ra, ve-hikah le-chal’ba, de-nashach le-shun’ra, de-achal le-gadya, di-z’van abba bi-t’rei zuzei, ḥad gadya, ḥad gadya. ,חוּטְרָא וְאָתָא ,לְכַלְבָּא וְהִכָּה ,לְשׁוּנְרָא דְּנָשַׁךְ ,לְגַדְיָא דְּאָכַל ,זוּזֵי בִּתְרֵי אַבָּא דִּזְבַן גַּדְיָא׃ חַד , גַּדְיָא חַד Then came a fire, that burnt the stick, that beat the dog, that ate the kid, that my father bought for two zuzim, ḥad gad’ya. Ve-ata nura, ve-saraf le-ḥut’ra, de-hikah le-chal’ba, de-nashach le-shun’ra, de-achal le-gadya, di-z’van abba bi-t’rei zuzei, ḥad gadya, ḥad gadya. ,נוּרָא וְאָתָא ,לְחוּטְרָא וְשָׂרַף ,לְכַלְבָּא דְּהִכָּה ,לְשׁוּנְרָא דְּנָשַׁךְ ,לְגַדְיָא דְּאָכַל ,זוּזֵי בִּתְרֵי אַבָּא דִּזְבַן גַּדְיָא׃ חַד , גַּדְיָא חַד Then came a water, that quenched the fire, that burnt the stick, that beat the dog, that ate the kid, that my father bought for two zuzim, ḥad gad’ya. Ve-ata maya, ve-chavah le-nura, de-saraf le-ḥut’ra, de-hikah le-chal’ba, de-nashach le-shun’ra, de-achal le-gadya, di-z’van abba bi-t’rei zuzei, ḥad gadya, ḥad gadya. ,מַיָּא וְאָתָא ,לְנוּרָא וְכָבָה ,לְחוּטְרָא דְּשָׂרַף ,לְכַלְבָּא דְּהִכָּה ,לְשׁוּנְרָא דְּנָשַׁךְ ,לְגַדְיָא דְּאָכַל ,זוּזֵי בִּתְרֵי אַבָּא דִּזְבַן גַּדְיָא׃ חַד , גַּדְיָא חַד ꔃ ~ 72 ~

exactText: Then came an ox, that drank the water, that quenched the fire, that burnt the stick, that beat the dog, that ate the kid, that my father bought for two zuzim, ḥad gad’ya. Ve-ata tora, ve-shatah le-maya, de-chavah le-nura, de-saraf le-ḥut’ra, de-hikah le-chal’ba, de-nashach le-shun’ra, de-achal le-gadya, di-z’van abba bi-t’rei zuzei, ḥad gadya, ḥad gadya. ,תוֹרָא וְאָתָא ,לְמַיָּא וְשָׁתָה ,לְנוּרָא דְּכָבָה ,לְחוּטְרָא דְּשָׂרַף ,לְכַלְבָּא דְּהִכָּה ,לְשׁוּנְרָא דְּנָשַׁךְ ,לְגַדְיָא דְּאָכַל ,זוּזֵי בִּתְרֵי אַבָּא דִּזְבַן גַּדְיָא׃ חַד , גַּדְיָא חַד Then came a slaughterer, who slaughtered the ox, that drank the water, that quenched the fire, that burnt the stick, that beat the dog, that ate the kid, that my father bought for two zuzim, ḥad gad’ya. Ve-ata shoḥeit, ve-shaḥat la-tora, de-shatah le-maya, de-chavah le-nura, de-saraf le-ḥut’ra, de-hikah le-chal’ba, de-nashach le-shun’ra, de-achal le-gadya, di-z’van abba bi-t’rei zuzei, ḥad gadya, ḥad gadya.

Ve-ata mal’ach ha-mavet, ve-shaḥat la-shoḥeit, de-shaḥat la-tora, de-shatah le-maya, de-chavah le-nura, de-saraf le-ḥut’ra, de-hikah le-chal’ba, de-nashach le-shun’ra, de-achal le-gadya, di-z’van abba bi-t’rei zuzei, ḥad gadya, ḥad gadya. ,הַמָּוֶת מַלְאַךְ וְאָתָא ,לַשׁוֹחֵט וְשָׁחַט ,לַתוֹרָא דְּשָׁחַט ,לְמַיָּא דְּשָׁתָה ,לְנוּרָא דְּכָבָה ,לְחוּטְרָא דְּשָׂרַף ,לְכַלְבָּא דְּהִכָּה ,לְשׁוּנְרָא דְּנָשַׁךְ ,לְגַדְיָא דְּאָכַל ,זוּזֵי בִּתְרֵי אַבָּא דִּזְבַן גַּדְיָא׃ חַד , גַּדְיָא חַד ꔃ ~ 73 ~

exactText: Then came the Holy One, who slew the Angel of Death, who slew the slaughterer, who slaughtered the ox, that drank the water, that quenched the fire, that burnt the stick, that beat the dog, that ate the kid, that my father bought for two zuzim, ḥad gad’ya. Ve-ata ha-kadosh baruch hu, ve-shaḥat la-mal’ach ha-mavet, de-shaḥat la-shoḥeit, de-shaḥat la-tora, de-shatah le-maya, de-chavah le-nura, de-saraf le-ḥut’ra, de-hikah le-chal’ba, de-nashach le-shun’ra, de-achal le-gadya, di-z’van abba bi-t’rei zuzei, ḥad gadya, ḥad gadya. ,הוּא בָּרוּךְ הַקָּדוֹשׁ וְאָתָא ,הַמָּוֶת לַמַלְאַךְ וְשָׁחַט ,לַשׁוֹחֵט דְּשָׁחַט ,לַתוֹרָא דְּשָׁחַט ,לְמַיָּא דְּשָׁתָה ,לְנוּרָא דְּכָבָה ,לְחוּטְרָא דְּשָׂרַף ,לְכַלְבָּא דְּהִכָּה ,לְשׁוּנְרָא דְּנָשַׁךְ ,לְגַדְיָא דְּאָכַל ,זוּזֵי בִּתְרֵי אַבָּא דִּזְבַן גַּדְיָא׃ חַד , גַּדְיָא חַד חסל&סידור&פסחThe seder is concluded ,כְּהִלְכָתוֹ פֶּֽסַח סִדּוּר חֲסַל .וְחֻקָּתוֹ כְּכׇל־מִשְׁפָּטוֹ ,אוֹתוֹ לְסַדֵּר זָכִֽינוּ כַּאֲשֶׁר לַﬠֲשׂוֹתוֹ׃ נִזְכֶּה כֵּן The seder has concluded as it should, according to its various laws and customs.

exactText: As we have merited to celebrate it now, may we do so again in future years.97 Ḥasal siddur pesaḥ ke-hil’chato, ke-chol mishpato ve-ḥukato. Ka’asher zachinu lesader oto, kein niz’keh la’asoto. בִּירוּשָׁלָֽיִם׃ הַבָּאָה לַשָּׁנָה Next year, in Jerusalem! Le-shanah ha-ba’ah bi-yerushalayim! ̛ Why do we end the seder with this particular wish? This is not a wish to uproot ourselves, or, God forbid, to uproot other people by moving ourselves. Rather, “Jerusalem, the rebuilt” represents an ideal, something we strive to build by our lives. The Hebrew word for Egypt is מִצְרָיִם mitz’rayim, which literally means “straits”. The name of Jerusalem יְרוּשָׁלָיִם yerushalayim derives in part from the word שָׁלֵם shalem, meaning “complete”, the root of שָׁלוֹם shalom, “peace”. ̛ Tonight, we have taken a step out of the narrow places, the places of despair and hopelessness, into the places of wholeness and peacefulness. We commit ourselves to building world characterized by an end to oppression, a world of tikkun olam, true liberation, ﬁlled with people who love their neighbors as they love themselves. This world will not magically spring into existence: we must build it ourselves, because nobody will do it for us. May this be our sacred task. 97.Rabbi Yosef Tur Elam (d. 1040) ~ 74 ~

exactText: [PDF p. 36, Shulchan Orech; visually checked and normalized from the PDF’s fi ligatures] So far, everything we have eaten in the course of the seder has had some ritual or symbolic significance. But one of the mitzvot of the holiday is to celebrate, and one of the traditional things we do to celebrate is partake of a festive meal. The ritual nature of the food of the meal is simply that it is there to be enjoyed, which itself is a mitzvah.
